reviews.review_msg: Random assignments posted a couple days before exams. Tas don't grade most things until end of semester. Lectures get sidetracked. Unclear instructions for assignments. Unhelpful and lackluster feedback on things that do get graded. If you're thinking of taking this course for a GPA boost, it's more trouble than it's worth.

took as an elective, put very little effort but still managed to stay at a consistent 90% all throughout the semester. lots of discussions and small assignments which can pile up, but just make sure to check assignments frequently. she shows a clear passion for the subject, would recommend her if you genuinely care about the topic!

I took Geog104 as a science credit, and overall would say it's a decent choice. Professor Pearson is incredibly kind and cares a lot about student success, however there a lot of assignments to keep up with, and the TAs can be very slow returning marks. Overall, a decent choice, as long as you stay on top of the weekly tasks.

I took Geo104 for one of my science credits. I recommend taking her class. She is very caring and passionate about what she teaches. Her lectures are long for sure, but all the slides are posted beforehand. There are a lot of assignments, but very manageable!

She is an amazing teacher. Her classes are interesting because she explains the topics clearly and encourages interaction. She communicates effectively and provides straightforward information. Her sense of humor makes her class even more enjoyable, and her exams are well-designed.
She is not understanding when it comes to your grade, and if you need help forget it she won't reply to your emails for at least 4 days.

little to no feedback is given. Takes a long time to respond to discussion/emails which is frustrating. timed quizzes were a pain if you asked her for more time she doesn't do anything. You can't contact TAs and if you contact her expect nothing but a reply saying she can't do anything . also don't expect her to spell your name right in emails.
